deck: Three Dimagi product leaders behind CommCare and SureAdhere on what it really takes to build SaaS for global health: moving from donor-driven projects to a sustainable product model, and why product marketing matters as much as engineering.
seo_description: Dimagi product leaders on building SaaS for global health: shifting from donor-funded projects to a sustainable product model with CommCare and SureAdhere.
og_description: Dimagi product leaders on building sustainable SaaS for global health with CommCare and SureAdhere.
about: A follow-up to Dimagi's earlier conversation on taking a product approach, this episode brings together three product leaders building the company's two most mature offerings, CommCare and SureAdhere. They dig into why global health needs software as a service, and why that model is as challenging as it is important in resource-constrained settings.

The discussion traces the move from project-oriented, donor-driven funding to a product operating model centered on user needs, sustainability, and scale. Along the way the group weighs hard trade-offs between maintaining existing software and chasing new features, the discipline of separating product revenue from project revenue, and the surprisingly central role of product marketing in helping customers see themselves in the product.

Hosted by Jonathan Jackson and Amie Vaccaro, the conversation closes with lightning-round lessons on building great products and teams: a clear North Star metric, the power of small focused teams, and good work sustained over a long period of time.

pull_quote.text: People don't want a product that can do anything. They want a product that can do their thing. And so, creating those stories that people can recognize themselves in, I think that's a critical missing piece.

bullets: Why global health needs SaaS: software you build once and serve many times, where more users lowers the cost per person reached
From projects to products: moving off donor-driven, project-based funding into a sustainable product operating model
Software rots: why building a product is only the start, and maintenance, security, and new features never stop
The maintain vs. build trade-off: making disciplined choices with thin margins and a revenue-sized team
Aligning revenue and impact: using a single North Star metric like patient adherence on SureAdhere to guide investment
Product marketing as the missing piece: helping a champion's local teams crest the hill and see themselves in CommCare
Track product revenue honestly: separating true recurring product revenue from project revenue so you don't fool yourself

stats.label: time Danny Roberts has worked at Dimagi
more customers Danny believes CommCare could support today
employees Instagram had when acquired for a billion dollars, cited as proof small teams can scale

guests.name: Danny Roberts
guests.role: Longtime engineer and product leader overseeing platform and product development on Dimagi's SaaS, after about 14 years at the company.

guests.name: Kai Cowger
guests.role: Product and technical leader on CommCare, who began as a field user and customer support before moving to the product side.

guests.name: Matthew Hayto
guests.role: Director of product on SureAdhere, focused on driving patient adherence as the measure of customer success.
